config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikalahkan begitu saja atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena justru perlindungan terhadap individu adalah dasar agar masyarakat tetap adil dan tidak sewenang-wenang. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai tanpa mengorbankan hak dasar orang per orang secara berlebihan. Dalam praktiknya, saya lebih percaya pada keseimbangan yang tegas: masyarakat kuat kalau individu juga dihormati.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the individual, because in the United States we tend to treat individual rights as the default limit on what society or government can demand. That said, I do not think rights are absolute in every case: public safety, disease control, and fair laws can justify some limits when they are narrowly tailored and accountable. What I reject is the idea that “society” is a blank check to override people’s freedoms whenever it is convenient or popular. A healthy society is one that protects the common good without turning individual rights into something optional.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman hidup di Indonesia, saya justru melihat bahaya ketika nama “kepentingan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kuasa, pembungkaman kritik, atau perlakuan tidak adil terhadap minoritas. Hak individu memang bisa dibatasi, tetapi hanya untuk alasan yang benar-benar jelas seperti keselamatan publik dan harus lewat aturan yang ketat, bukan karena mayoritas merasa lebih nyaman. Masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi keduanya secara seimbang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From a U.S. perspective, that idea sets off alarms because it can easily become a justification for majoritarian abuse, especially when “society” is used as a vague excuse to silence unpopular people or weaken civil liberties. At the same time, I do not buy the absolute version of individual rights either, because public health, safety, and equal laws sometimes require limits that are clear, narrow, and accountable. The real test is whether a society can protect the common good without treating individual rights as disposable whenever they become inconvenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu karena di Indonesia, “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai sebagai alasan untuk menekan yang lemah atau membungkam yang berbeda. Hak individu memang bisa dibatasi, tetapi harus sangat ketat, jelas tujuannya, dan tidak boleh jadi pembenaran untuk kesewenang-wenangan mayoritas atau negara. Buat saya, masyarakat yang baik bukan yang paling gampang mengorbankan individu demi slogan kebersamaan, melainkan yang mampu menjaga ketertiban tanpa menghapus martabat dan kebebasan orang per orang. Jadi, prioritas utama seharusnya bukan masyarakat di atas individu, melainkan keseimbangan yang adil dengan perlindungan hak dasar sebagai batas yang tegas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the claim that society should generally outrank the individual, because from a U.S. perspective that is exactly how civil liberties get eroded by majorities, governments, or “for the common good” rhetoric. At the same time, I do not think individual rights are a blank check: laws on public safety, fraud, and disease control can limit freedom when the limits are specific, necessary, and accountable. What I reject is the idea that “society” is automatically more legitimate than the person, since the whole point of rights is to protect people when the crowd or the state wants to push too far. A good society serves the common good by respecting individual dignity first, not by treating that dignity as expendable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
